summary: Preinstall hook delegates telemetry collection to @doaction/shared
details: package.json declares a preinstall script (`node scripts/postinstall.js`) that require()s `@doaction/shared/bin/postinstall.js` from a declared dependency (`@doaction/shared` ^9.9.9). The README states this triggers Datadog environment telemetry on `npm install`. This tarball itself contains no environment reads, no network I/O, and no obfuscation — the installer-side effect is determined entirely by what `@doaction/shared` does at install time. Routing for human review so the dependency's preinstall behavior can be assessed: whether the collected fields are mild PII vs. credentials/env scraping, whether the destination is the publisher's own telemetry endpoint vs. an attacker-controlled host, and whether opt-out/disclosure is present. The auto-execution at install with no opt-in is a consent concern regardless of the dependency's content (breaks air-gapped/CI environments).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
